key_insight: SimPhysNet uses physics-informed self-supervised learning plus few-shot prototypical classification to predict laser welding penetration accurately with very little labeled data.
engineering_relevance: This enables more scalable, data-efficient weld quality monitoring and supports intelligent automation for defect-free laser welding.
method: Physics-informed neural network (PINN) embeds physical priors into contrastive self-supervised feature learning.
Three image augmentation tasks improve robustness and generalization from unlabeled molten-pool/keyhole images.
Prototypical networks perform few-shot classification using only a small labeled set.
evidence_signal: The model reached 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, comparable to fully supervised baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the availability of representative unlabeled welding images and the reliability of the physics priors under changing process conditions.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
